content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be redesigned to treat "unused, low-relevance stored memories" the same way a curious reinforcement learning agent treats unvisited environment states, prioritizing memory re-evaluation for active learning through intrinsic reward. This re-prioritization solves the common problem of stale, underutilized cached knowledge that active learning agents forget to revisit to update with new task information.
